mappings.rationaleAI: Core VSI for this section, covering nutritional patterns, essential nutrients (A.3), diet, metabolism, vitamins, and protein requirements of organisms.
Addresses the procurement and processing of nutrients, food composition, and nutritional requirements relevant to heterotrophic nutrition (A.1).
Covers photosynthesis and autotrophic nutrition (A.1, B), the primary mechanism for food production in green plants.
Addresses the human digestive system including control of secretions and intestinal movements (C.2.d), and physiological processing of nutrients.
Covers anatomical structures of the vertebrate digestive system (C.2.b): oral cavity, esophagus, stomach, intestines, pancreas, and liver.
Covers the diversity of invertebrate digestive systems (C.2.a) from vacuolar to tubular systems across animal phyla.
Directly relevant to digestion (C), covering digestive enzymes and their role in the chemical breakdown and processing of nutrients.
Covers teeth as part of the oral cavity in vertebrate digestive systems (C.2.b), relevant to methods of ingestion and feeding adaptations.
Directly relevant to syntrophism (A.4), covering nutritional interrelationships where metabolic products of one organism nourish another, including microbiome and plant root interactions.
Covers nutritional roles of microbial communities in digestion and nutrient processing, directly relevant to syntrophism (A.4) and gut-based nutrient procurement.
Addresses diverse nutritional patterns (A.1) including chemotrophism and autotrophism in bacteria, archaea, and fungi, plus nitrogen fixation as a syntrophic process.
Covers applied aspects of nutrition and food production, including soil nutrients and the agricultural basis of human nutrient procurement.
